Title: PpMYC2 and PpJAM2/3 antagonistically regulate lignin synthesis to cope with the disease in peach fruit

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core lignin monolignol pathway entry step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to form trans-cinnamic acid, providing entry flux into the phenylpropanoid/lignin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key upstream control step for lignin precursor supply; not explicitly stated as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to resistance against fungal disease infection.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection/inoculation site; Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher PpPAL1 expression is associated with increased lignin accumulation during disease response; activated by PpMYC2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient overexpression and silencing in peach fruit; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient PpMYC2 overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PpPAL1 promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC reporter; effectors driven by CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with upstream positive regulator PpMYC2 to enhance lignin-based disease resistance in postharvest peach fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpPAL1 was identified as a PpMYC2 target by combined DAP-seq and RNA-seq. 2. PpMYC2 activated PpPAL1 promoter activity and increased its expression in peach fruit. 3. Its induction correlated with increased lignin accumulation during fungal infection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpPAL1 was not reported as a direct repression target of PpJAM2 or PpJAM3 in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to produce p-coumaric acid in the early phenylpropanoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key lignin synthesis gene; not explicitly established as rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection/inoculation site; Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation promotes lignin accumulation; activated by PpMYC2 and repressed by PpJAM2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient overexpression and silencing in peach fruit; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient TF overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PpC4H promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC reporter; effectors driven by CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful lignin-pathway node for engineering disease resistance, especially with enhancing PpMYC2 and reducing PpJAM2 repression.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpC4H was a direct common target of PpMYC2 and PpJAM2. 2. PpMYC2 activated, whereas PpJAM2 repressed, PpC4H transcription. 3. PpJAM2 interfered with PpMYC2 activation through competitive promoter binding, affecting lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Arabidopsis, homologous AtC4H expression changed consistently with MYC2/JAM2 manipulations.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to their CoA thioesters, enabling entry into downstream monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms activated precursors for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key lignin synthesis gene; not explicitly established as rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection/inoculation site; Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation promotes lignin accumulation; activated by PpMYC2 and repressed by PpJAM3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient overexpression and silencing in peach fruit; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient TF overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native Pp4CL1 promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC reporter; effectors driven by CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for lignin-mediated resistance engineering in combination with PpMYC2 activation and PpJAM3 reduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pp4CL1 was identified as a direct target of PpMYC2 and a repression target of PpJAM3. 2. PpMYC2 activated whereas PpJAM3 suppressed Pp4CL1 transcription. 3. Competitive binding between PpMYC2 and PpJAM3 modulated lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its promoter contained a PpMYC2-binding motif identified by DAP-seq.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes caffeoyl shikimate to caffeate in the monolignol biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms intermediate precursor for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key lignin synthesis gene; not explicitly established as rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection/inoculation site; Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation promotes lignin accumulation; activated by PpMYC2 and repressed by PpJAM2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient overexpression and silencing in peach fruit; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient TF overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PpCSE promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC reporter; effectors driven by CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targetable node for boosting lignin formation and disease resistance, particularly by counteracting PpJAM2 repression.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCSE was directly activated by PpMYC2 and directly repressed by PpJAM2. 2. Its expression increased during fungal infection together with lignin accumulation. 3. PpJAM2 competitively interfered with PpMYC2 activation on the PpCSE promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression of the Arabidopsis homolog AtCSE followed the same trend in mutant/complementation assays.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates caffeoyl-CoA to feruloyl-CoA, a key intermediate in monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate precursor for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key lignin synthesis gene; not explicitly established as rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and contributes to fungal disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection/inoculation site; Arabidopsis leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 6, 12 and 24 h after infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulation promotes lignin accumulation; activated by PpMYC2 and repressed by PpJAM3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient overexpression and silencing in peach fruit; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient TF overexpression or silencing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PpCCoAOMT1 promoter in pGreenII 0800-LUC reporter; effectors driven by CaMV 35S
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suitable downstream lignin-pathway engineering target, especially with PpMYC2 enhancement or PpJAM3 attenuation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpCCoAOMT1 was directly activated by PpMYC2 and directly repressed by PpJAM3. 2. Its expression was induced during fungal infection together with lignin accumulation. 3. PpJAM3 competitively antagonized PpMYC2 activation at the promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study positions PpCCoAOMT1 as one of the critical PpMYC2-responsive lignin genes in disease defence.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with basic region and HLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major positive regulator of lignin accumulation and disease resistance in peach fruit during Monilinia fructicola infection
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and helps defend fruit against fungal infection.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection site; Arabidopsis leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to Monilinia fructicola.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis through activation of phenylpropanoid pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates PpPAL1, PpC4H, Pp4CL1, PpCSE and PpCCoAOMT1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated early after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 12 and 24 h, then decreases by 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated early after Monilinia fructicola infection; high at 12 and 24 h, then decreases by 48 h
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression in pART-CAM; native target promoters in DLR reporters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator for improving postharvest disease resistance through lignin enhancement; may be combined with suppression/editing of PpJAM2/3 for stronger effect.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; lesion assay; RT-qPCR; DAP-seq; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; Arabidopsis mutant complementation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYC2 expression was induced by Monilinia fructicola infection. 2. PpMYC2 positively regulated peach and Arabidopsis resistance by promoting lignin accumulation. 3. It directly activated five lignin biosynthetic genes by binding their promoters. 4. Its activation on shared targets was antagonized by PpJAM2/3 through competitive promoter binding.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpMYC2 was phylogenetically closest to SlMYC2 and functioned as a nuclear protein.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor with basic region and HLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via direct promoter binding and competitive antagonism of PpMYC2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator that suppresses lignin accumulation and weakens disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strengthens cell walls and helps defend fruit against fungal infection.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit pulp near infection site; Arabidopsis leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces lignin accumulation and decreases resistance to Monilinia fructicola when overexpressed; silencing has the opposite effect.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses PpC4H and PpCSE; interferes with PpMYC2 activation of shared targets
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Down-regulated from 6 h after Monilinia fructicola inoculation and remains low thereafter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated from 6 h after Monilinia fructicola inoculation and remains low thereafter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for overexpression in pART-CAM; native target promoters in DLR reporters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for gene editing or silencing to increase lignin-based postharvest disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; lesion assay; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; Arabidopsis mutant complementation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpJAM2 expression decreased after Monilinia fructicola infection. 2. PpJAM2 negatively regulated disease resistance and lignin accumulation. 3. It directly repressed PpC4H and PpCSE. 4. PpJAM2 antagonized PpMYC2 by competing for the same promoter binding sites.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests weaker/less conserved acidic activation domain relative to PpMYC2 may underlie repressor behavior.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses Pp4CL1 and PpCCoAOMT1; interferes with PpMYC2 activation of shared targets

Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Monilinia fructicola infection; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for suppression/editing to enhance lignin-mediated resistance in peach fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient overexpression and VIGS in peach fruit; lesion assay; dual-luciferase reporter assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; competitive EMSA; Arabidopsis mutant complementation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpJAM3 expression decreased after Monilinia fructicola infection. 2. PpJAM3 negatively regulated disease resistance and lignin accumulation. 3. It directly repressed Pp4CL1 and PpCCoAOMT1. 4. PpJAM3 antagonized PpMYC2 by competing for the same promoter binding sites.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PpJAM3 was phylogenetically closest to SlMTB3 and showed nuclear localization.
